text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously risk-on. The real driver is the AI trade narrative - headlines suggest chipmakers are still getting bought despite Mag 7 valuation pressure. That's directly relevant to your book, which is ~90% tech/chips. The AI spending jitter story is a two-sided knife: sentiment can flip fast.

Your holdings: SOXL (-7.06%) is the one to watch - it's a 3x leveraged semiconductor ETF and decay plus margin make this a compounding risk if chips stall; stop is $200, respect it hard. SPCX (+0.53%) is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history - margin amplifies any gap-down risk here, manage it manually and keep size small. SMH (-0.69%) is mildly underwater but not alarming yet; it follows SOXL's fate directionally.

Watch: How chip names react to the Mag 7 valuation story today - if SMH starts rolling over, SOXL's stop at $200 becomes urgent. Also watch CRWD; it's your best performer and the scanner sees a clean long setup.

Bottom line: Protect SOXL and SPCX first - everything else can wait.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your line in the sand - with 3x leverage and margin, do not let this become a bigger loss; tighten mentally to $215-220 if you want extra cushion given the -7% hole you're already in.

CRWD: Already up 3.26% and scanner shows grade-A long bias with entry 741.42-742.17, stop 740.31 - if it holds that zone, fine to let it ride; no need to add here since you already own it and price is in premium.
No new buys today - ALAB and AMAT have clean scanner setups but no specific catalyst from today's news to justify adding margin exposure on top of what you're carrying.
